task_completion.description: Export the front elevation view as a JPEG
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the DreamPlan interface showing a flat, front-facing elevation view of the house, and an export/save dialog or file explorer showing 'elevation_front.jpg' being saved to the Desktop.

task_completion.description: Export the side elevation view as a JPEG
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the DreamPlan interface showing a flat, side-facing elevation view of the house, and an export/save dialog or file explorer showing 'elevation_side.jpg' being saved to the Desktop.

task_completion.description: Export the 2D ground floor plan as a JPEG
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the DreamPlan interface in 2D blueprint/floor plan mode (top-down view), and an export/save dialog or file explorer showing 'construction_floor_plan.jpg' being saved to the Desktop.

task_completion.description: Export the 3D overview perspective as a JPEG
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the DreamPlan interface in a 3D angled perspective view, and an export/save dialog or file explorer showing 'construction_overview.jpg' being saved to the Desktop.

task_completion.description: Save the project file to the Documents folder
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a 'Save Project' or 'Save As' dialog in DreamPlan showing the file being saved as 'construction_docs.dpn' in the C:\Users\Docker\Documents directory.

integrity.description: Agent used the DreamPlan GUI to export images
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the agent clicking through DreamPlan's menus (e.g., File -> Export, or view toggles) rather than using terminal commands to generate or move files.

integrity.description: Agent genuinely manipulated the camera and view modes
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the DreamPlan viewport changing between 2D, 3D, and elevation modes, confirming the agent didn't just rename existing files or download images from the internet.

integrity.description: Agent did not spoof the files using a text editor or touch command
integrity.visual_evidence: The files must be created via the software's export/save functionality, not by typing 'touch elevation_front.jpg' or saving empty files in Notepad.

privileged_info_for_vlm: DreamPlan Home Design Software supports distinct 2D, 3D, and elevation view modes. The agent must navigate these modes to capture the 4 required views. Note a discrepancy in the task: it asks to save the project as 'construction_docs.dpn', but DreamPlan's native project format is '.ddp'. The agent should still attempt to save the file with the requested '.dpn' extension or name in the Documents folder. Verify that the agent actually uses the DreamPlan GUI to export the images, as the system requires the images to be valid and >= 10,000 bytes.
